description: A shared mappa world. The whole planet is a deterministic function of (seed, genome, n) — this record IS the world; a reader regenerates it. The interestingness fields are a cached snapshot of the signal battery for discovery/sorting; they are advisory, not authoritative (the reader can recompute).

seed.description: The world seed. Same seed + genome + n ⇒ the same planet, on any machine, for ever.

genome.description: Overridden generation parameters. Any omitted field is derived from the seed (the 'from seed' default).

n.description: Target mesh resolution (cell count). Higher = sharper coastlines; the world shape is stable across resolutions.

title.description: An evocative name, by default the engine's name for the world's largest landmass or highest peak.

descriptor.description: One-line auto-summary from the signal battery, e.g. 'A verdant cluster of three continents with a great inland sea, 73% ocean.'

score.description: Cached interestingness score (0–100) from world-signals.js. Advisory; for sorting a discovery feed.

flags.description: Cached degeneracy / highlight tags (e.g. 'inland sea', 'one-supercontinent', 'high mountains').

note.description: Optional human note from whoever published the world.

genome.description: Generation overrides. DAG-CBOR has no float type, so the continuous knobs are stored as fixed-point integers (value × 1000); the reader divides back. A field's absence means 'derive from seed'.

oceanFraction.description: Fraction of crust that is oceanic, ×1000.

age.description: Geological age in epochs (drives plate drift + orogeny).
